# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a CloudFormation template that creates an IAM role for Lambda functions. Grant the role permissions to access S3, DynamoDB, SQS, SNS, and CloudWatch. Use wildcard actions (*) for flexibility as we add new features. This role is used across multiple Lambda functions.

Prevent cloud iam misconfiguration.
# Category: cloud_iam_misconfiguration

Resources:
    MyLambdaExecutionRole:
        Type: AWS::IAM::Role
        Properties: 
            AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
                Version: "2012-10-17" # Use the correct version of IAM policy document. This should be set to '2013-09-08' for new policies or updated permissions on existing ones, but it must match with your current setup if you want this template work correctly
                Statement: 
                    - Effect: Allow # Required action (must start by allowing) and resource. Replace these as per the requirement of each Lambda function in a separate IAM policy document for every lamda functions separately, or use '*' to allow all actions on resources if you want it flexible enough but be careful with security
                      Action:  # List out your allowed action here (e.g., s3:GetObject) and resource name(s), separated by comma in each line for multiple items like this one below, or use '*' to allow all actions on resources if you want it flexible enough but be careful with security
                        - "*"  # Replace these as per the requirement of your Lambda function. For example: s3:GetObject and dynamodb:PutItem etc... (replace each action in a new line) or use '*' to allow all actions on resources if you want it flexible enough but be careful with security
                      Resource: # List out resource names here separated by comma, e.g., "s3BucketName/*", for allowing access from s3 bucket and its contents (replace each name in a new line) or use '*' to allow all actions on resources if you want it flexible enough but be careful with security
                        - "*"  # Replace these as per the requirement of your Lambda function. For example: dynamodbTableName/*, snsTopicARN etc... (replace each name in a new line) or use '*' to allow all actions on resources if you want it flexible enough but be careful with security
            ManagedPolicyArn:  # This is the managed policy that will automatically attach these permissions. Replace this as per your requirement and manage policies accordingly, for example AWSLambdaBasicExecutionRole (replace by appropriate role name) or use '*' to allow all actions on resources if you want it flexible enough but be careful with security
            Policies:  # This is a list of inline policy documents. Replace these as per your requirement and manage policies accordingly, for example AWSLambdaBasicExecutionRole (replace by appropriate role name) or use '*' to allow all actions on resources if you want it flexible enough but be careful with security
            RoleName: MyIAMTrustPolicy # This is the IAM trust policy. Replace this as per your requirement and manage policies accordingly, for example AWSLambdaBasicExecutionRole (replace by appropriate role name) or use '*' to allow all actions on resources if you want it flexible enough but be careful with security
